apps.usage: Compass Rose Ingenuity 5th graders get Amplify ELA support customized for their specific school context and student population. The tool bridges the gap between a national curriculum and local classroom needs.
apps.impact: Showed that national curriculum materials work best when they're locally adapted — Compass Rose students got Amplify's rigor with their school's personality baked in.

apps.name: [K-5 Outlines] IM Pathways Assistant
apps.usage: K-5 teachers see the big picture of the IM mathematical progression, understanding how their grade's work connects to what came before and what comes next. The tool helps teachers make informed decisions about pacing and emphasis.
apps.impact: Gave elementary teachers a bird's-eye view of the K-5 mathematical journey — teachers stopped teaching their grade in isolation and started seeing themselves as part of a coherent mathematical story.

apps.name: K-2 English Phonics Decodables
apps.usage: Teachers create decodable reading passages precisely aligned to the phonics skills each student is currently learning. Every passage matches where the student is in their phonics progression.
apps.impact: Ensured every K-2 reader practices with texts that match exactly where they are — no more frustration from texts that are too hard or boredom from texts they've outgrown.

apps.name: Decode Me
apps.usage: Students practice foundational decoding skills through interactive exercises that feel like puzzles rather than remedial work. The tool focuses on the specific phonics patterns each student needs to master.
apps.impact: Made decoding practice feel like a puzzle to solve rather than a label to carry — struggling readers engaged willingly because the format respected their dignity while building essential skills.

apps.impact: Transformed financial literacy from abstract to lived experience

apps.name: Critical Minds

apps.name: M1: TravelGuide
apps.description: Student travel research guide for geography and cultural exploration at Fairfax County Public Schools
apps.usage: Fairfax high school students research real travel destinations as part of their geography and cultural studies coursework. They explore logistics, culture, and history of different places, turning abstract map knowledge into vivid, personal understanding of the world.
apps.impact: Connected geography to real-world exploration in a way that textbooks never could — students who used to see maps as flat information started seeing them as invitations to understand how people live, eat, celebrate, and build communities around the world.

apps.name: Mindful Mastery
apps.description: SEL and mindfulness support tool for student wellbeing at Fairfax County Public Schools
apps.usage: Fairfax students practice mindfulness and stress management techniques through guided exercises and reflections. The tool provides concrete strategies for managing the pressure of a competitive academic environment, giving students skills they can use both in school and at home.
apps.impact: Gave Fairfax students concrete tools for managing stress and anxiety rather than just telling them to 'take a deep breath' — students who felt overwhelmed by academic pressure developed personal wellness routines that helped them perform better because they felt better.

apps.name: Equity Minded Inquiry
apps.description: Project-based equity inquiry tool for inclusive classroom practice at Fairfax County Public Schools
apps.usage: Fairfax students and teachers engage in equity-focused inquiry projects that examine real issues of inclusion, access, and representation in their school community. The tool structures the inquiry process so conversations about equity are productive rather than performative.
apps.impact: Pushed Fairfax classrooms toward more inclusive, equity-centered practice through student-led inquiry rather than top-down mandates — when students investigate equity themselves, the insights stick in ways that professional development workshops never achieve.

apps.name: Parent Leader Support 3000

apps.name: Wayside Schools Family Virtual Helper
apps.usage: Wayside families use this virtual helper to navigate school resources, find answers to common questions, and stay connected with their child's education — all without waiting for office hours. It serves as a 24/7 bridge between home and school.
apps.impact: Broke down the communication barriers that kept many Wayside families from engaging with their child's school — parents who felt intimidated by the system now have a welcoming, always-available guide.

apps.name: CIOB: ParentAssist Pro
apps.usage: Parents get help with school-home communication
apps.impact: Broke down communication barriers

apps.name: Multilingual Lovely Shout-outs
apps.usage: Teachers celebrate students in their home languages, creating moments of linguistic pride and belonging that affirm every student's identity. The tool generates shout-outs in students' native languages for classroom recognition and family communication.
apps.impact: Created moments of pure joy when students heard their accomplishments celebrated in the language of their home — a small gesture that sends the powerful message "your whole self is welcome here."

apps.name: DualLingoPal: Home-to-School Language Builder
apps.usage: Multilingual families bridge language gaps
apps.impact: Connected home and school languages showing linguistic assets are valued

apps.name: Murrow
apps.creator: Journalistic Learning
apps.usage: Students develop journalism skills through AI-guided practice — learning to research, interview, verify sources, and write compelling stories with the rigor of professional newsrooms. Murrow coaches students through the full journalistic process, from identifying a story angle to polishing a final draft.
apps.impact: The highest-engagement app on the platform — Murrow proved that AI can support sophisticated, creative disciplines like journalism at unprecedented scale. Students who had never written beyond a five-paragraph essay discovered they could produce real journalism that mattered to their communities.

apps.name: Year 1 - Mathematics - Subject Specific App
apps.description: Year 1 mathematics curriculum app for Ghana
apps.usage: Year 1 students build foundational math skills through curriculum-aligned content designed for Ghana's national standards. Teachers use it as a daily instructional resource for mathematical concepts.
apps.impact: Became Ghana's most-used curriculum app, proving that AI-powered tools can deliver quality math instruction at scale in contexts where textbook access and teacher training resources are limited.

apps.name: Sassy
apps.description: Helps Oregon students explore career and trade pathways
apps.creator: Oregon Dept of Education / JLI
apps.usage: Oregon students explore career and trade pathways with AI guidance tailored to their state's specific opportunities, apprenticeship programs, and workforce needs. The tool connects classroom learning to real career options available in their region.
apps.impact: Brought state-level career guidance directly to students — instead of generic advice, Oregon students got pathways matched to their state's actual economy, apprenticeships, and employer needs.

apps.name: Year 2 - Ghanaian Language - Asante Twi
apps.description: Asante Twi language instruction for Year 2 students
apps.usage: Year 2 students learn Asante Twi through curriculum-aligned lessons that preserve and teach Ghana's indigenous language. The tool makes mother-tongue instruction accessible and engaging for young learners building their linguistic foundation.
apps.impact: Demonstrated that AI can support indigenous language preservation and instruction — students learning Asante Twi gained a digital companion that honored their cultural heritage while building literacy in their mother tongue.

apps.name: JiJi Resource Scout
apps.description: AI tool for finding ST Math lessons and resources
apps.creator: One8 Foundation / ST Math Partnership
apps.usage: Teachers quickly find and align ST Math lessons and resources to their instructional goals. The tool navigates the extensive ST Math library to surface the right visual math puzzles and activities for specific concepts and standards.
apps.impact: Unlocked the full potential of ST Math by making its vast library navigable — teachers who only used a fraction of available resources discovered lessons perfectly matched to the concepts their students were struggling with.

apps.name: Project Launcher
apps.description: The original PBL project design tool
apps.usage: Teachers design and launch project-based learning experiences with AI support that helps them scope projects, define driving questions, plan milestones, and create authentic assessments.
apps.impact: Lowered the barrier to PBL adoption by giving teachers a structured starting point — educators who felt overwhelmed by open-ended project design gained the confidence to launch ambitious, student-centered projects.

apps.name: CLEMENT LEARNING PLATFORM
apps.description: Comprehensive learning platform for basic schools in Ghana
apps.usage: Students and teachers in Ghana's basic schools access a comprehensive learning platform that supports instruction across multiple subjects aligned to the national curriculum. The tool provides structured content and interactive exercises that supplement classroom teaching.
apps.impact: Brought digital learning resources to basic schools across Ghana where physical materials are scarce — students gained access to consistent, high-quality content regardless of their school's resource level.

apps.name: AI's Impact on Your Career
apps.description: Explores AI's impact on career fields
apps.creator: Mike Taubman
apps.usage: Students explore how AI is transforming specific career fields they're interested in, learning which skills will become more valuable and which tasks will be automated. The tool turns abstract AI anxiety into concrete, personalized career planning.
apps.impact: Shifted the student conversation about AI from fear to strategy — instead of worrying that robots will take their jobs, students started planning how to build the skills that make them irreplaceable in an AI-augmented workforce.

apps.name: RACES
apps.description: RACES writing strategy with rubric-aligned feedback
apps.creator: Lars Nelson
apps.usage: Students practice the RACES writing strategy (Restate, Answer, Cite, Explain, Summarize) with AI feedback aligned to their teacher's rubric. The tool coaches students through each step of constructing evidence-based written responses.
apps.impact: Gave students a structured framework that made evidence-based writing feel achievable rather than overwhelming — students who froze at open-ended prompts started producing organized, well-supported responses with confidence.

apps.name: Re-Imagining Migration
apps.description: Helps educators build inclusive communities for diverse populations
apps.creator: Adam Strom
apps.usage: Educators use this tool to develop culturally responsive practices for welcoming and supporting students from migrant and immigrant backgrounds. It helps teachers build inclusive classroom communities that honor diverse experiences and perspectives.
apps.impact: Equipped educators with the language and strategies to turn classrooms into genuinely welcoming spaces — immigrant students who felt invisible started feeling seen, and their classmates gained empathy and global understanding.

apps.name: Job Finder
apps.usage: Ridgewood NJ students explore career paths by entering their interests, skills, and curiosities, then receiving personalized job matches with descriptions of what the work actually looks like day-to-day. The tool turns abstract "what do you want to be when you grow up" conversations into concrete, research-backed exploration.
apps.impact: Opened students' eyes to career paths they didn't know existed — many discovered that their unique combination of interests mapped to real jobs they'd never heard of. Students who felt directionless gained confidence that there was a place for them in the working world.

apps.name: Mr. Fransen's ESL Teacher Assistant
apps.usage: ESL teachers get classroom support and resource generation tailored to their students' proficiency levels. The tool captures Mr. Fransen's teaching expertise and makes it available to students anytime.
apps.impact: Gave an individual teacher's best practices a way to scale — Mr. Fransen's teaching wisdom became available to ESL students anytime, extending quality instruction beyond the classroom walls.

apps.name: KIPP's Toni - A Virtual Writing TA
apps.description: KIPP's flagship virtual writing teaching assistant
apps.usage: Students across KIPP schools get writing feedback and coaching from Toni, a virtual TA that helps them improve their essays, arguments, and creative writing with personalized, actionable guidance.
apps.impact: Scaled high-quality writing feedback across KIPP's network — students who previously had to wait days for teacher comments could iterate on their writing in real time, building confidence and skill simultaneously.

apps.name: Corbin, Your Feedback Sidekick
apps.creator: Thomas Gaffey
apps.usage: Teachers and students use Corbin to get competency-aligned feedback on student work evidence. The tool evaluates student submissions against specific competency indicators and provides targeted, actionable feedback that helps students understand exactly where they stand and what to work on next.
apps.impact: Made competency-based feedback feel personal and immediate rather than abstract and delayed — students who used to see rubrics as mysterious scoring tools started seeing them as roadmaps for growth.

apps.name: Model UN Simulator
apps.creator: Molly Harris
apps.usage: Students simulate Model United Nations debates, taking on country roles, researching geopolitical positions, and negotiating resolutions with AI delegates. The tool brings the complexity and diplomacy of international relations into any classroom, regardless of whether the school has a formal MUN program.
apps.impact: Democratized access to Model UN — students at schools without dedicated programs got the same experience in critical thinking, diplomacy, and global awareness that elite prep school students have had for decades.

apps.name: Sullivan HS Virtual Tutor
apps.description: High school math tutoring app for Sullivan HS students
apps.usage: Sullivan High School students get on-demand math tutoring support that meets them at their level, helping them work through problems step-by-step with patient, personalized guidance.
apps.impact: Provided consistent math support outside of class hours — students who struggled in silence at home finally had a patient tutor available whenever they needed help, closing the gap between school and independent practice.

apps.name: Instructional Strategy Generator
apps.description: Generates instructional strategies for all learners
apps.creator: Niesha Butler
apps.usage: Teachers input their lesson objectives and student needs, then receive tailored instructional strategies that address diverse learning profiles. The tool helps educators differentiate instruction without spending hours researching pedagogical approaches.
apps.impact: Closed the gap between knowing students need differentiation and actually knowing how to do it — teachers who felt stuck using one-size-fits-all approaches gained a repertoire of strategies matched to their specific students.

apps.name: College Success Thought Partner
apps.description: AI thought partner for college success
apps.creator: Andrea Walker
apps.usage: Students navigating college use this AI thought partner to work through academic challenges, study strategies, time management, and the social-emotional aspects of higher education. The tool provides the kind of mentorship that first-generation students often lack.
apps.impact: Gave college students — especially first-generation ones — the always-available mentor that their more privileged peers take for granted. Students who felt like they didn't belong gained a thought partner that helped them see they absolutely did.

apps.name: Inventor Bot
apps.usage: Students work through the engineering design process with AI guidance — identifying problems, brainstorming solutions, prototyping ideas, and iterating on designs. The bot coaches young inventors through each stage of turning a creative idea into a workable invention.
apps.impact: Gave students permission to think like engineers and inventors — kids who assumed inventing was for geniuses discovered that the design process is a learnable skill, and their ideas were worth developing.

apps.name: Draft iCivics App - with Knowledge Graph
apps.description: Civics education app powered by knowledge graph technology
apps.usage: Students explore civics concepts through an AI-powered knowledge graph that connects ideas across government, citizenship, and civic participation in ways that reveal the deeper structure of democratic institutions.
apps.impact: Showcased cutting-edge knowledge graph technology applied to civic education — a preview of where AI in education is heading, connecting concepts in ways that make civics feel interconnected rather than fragmented.

apps.name: KIPP NC IM Lesson Preparation and Internalization Coach
apps.description: Math lesson internalization coach for KIPP NC teachers using Illustrative Mathematics
apps.usage: KIPP NC teachers prepare and internalize IM math lessons with coaching support that helps them understand the mathematical storyline, anticipate student thinking, and plan purposeful questions.
apps.impact: Transformed lesson planning from a solo activity into a coached conversation — teachers stopped just reading the lesson guide and started truly internalizing the mathematical goals and pedagogical moves.

apps.name: EmiWatch Emissions Tracker
apps.description: Tracks environmental impact and suggests actions
apps.creator: Emi
apps.usage: Students track their environmental impact through daily activity logging, receiving personalized insights about their carbon footprint and actionable suggestions for reducing emissions. The app connects abstract climate science to concrete personal choices.
apps.impact: A student-built app that turned climate anxiety into climate agency — young people stopped feeling helpless about the environment and started seeing how their individual choices connect to collective impact.

apps.name: Dynamic Coaching Toolbox
apps.creator: JD
apps.usage: NYCPS educators access a dynamic library of student-centered classroom protocols matched to their specific instructional goals. The tool recommends discussion structures, collaborative routines, and engagement strategies based on what the teacher is trying to achieve.
apps.impact: Transformed coaching conversations from theoretical to practical — instead of vague advice to 'be more student-centered,' teachers got specific protocols they could implement in their very next class period.

apps.name: CMU Pathway Bot
apps.creator: Jolene Martin
apps.usage: Students exploring construction and maintenance career pathways get 24/7 guidance on course sequences, certifications, job prospects, and skill requirements. The bot helps students navigate a complex vocational landscape with personalized recommendations.
apps.impact: Made skilled trades pathways as well-mapped as traditional college routes — students who felt lost in vocational program options gained a clear, personalized roadmap to high-demand careers.

apps.name: CSUCI EU Mahi-Bot
apps.description: Personalized student assistance for university navigation
apps.creator: Jerilee Perlata
apps.usage: CSU Channel Islands students navigate university resources, academic requirements, and campus services through Mahi-Bot, a personalized AI assistant. The tool serves as a 24/7 guide for students who might otherwise struggle to find the right office or resource.
apps.impact: Reduced the intimidation factor of navigating a large university system — first-generation college students who didn't know where to start finally had a patient guide that met them where they were.

apps.name: Community Superheroes
apps.creator: Students in the Bronx
apps.usage: Bronx students research and tell stories about unsung heroes in their local communities — the crossing guard, the bodega owner, the neighborhood organizer. The app turns community storytelling into a creative, student-driven project.
apps.impact: Helped students see their own neighborhoods as places full of heroism and worth celebrating — kids who felt their community had nothing special discovered extraordinary people living ordinary lives right next door.

apps.name: National Math AI Partnership
apps.creator: National Math AI Partnership
apps.usage: Math teachers across the partnership use AI coaching tools to plan and internalize high-quality instructional materials. The suite of apps supports lesson preparation, student thinking anticipation, and instructional decision-making aligned to HQIM standards.
apps.impact: Created a national model for how AI can enhance — not replace — high-quality math curriculum implementation, proving that technology and rigorous pedagogy amplify each other when thoughtfully combined.

apps.name: Personalized Lesson Generator (Kingsman Academy, DC)
apps.description: AI-powered lesson generator aligned to Kingsman Academy's mastery-based learning model, creating individualized learning plans
apps.creator: Kingsman Academy
apps.usage: Teachers create individualized learning plans aligned to Kingsman Academy's mastery-based model, reducing hours of manual planning while maintaining true personalization for every student. The tool generates differentiated content that meets each student exactly where they are.
apps.impact: Made true mastery-based personalization feasible for every teacher at Kingsman — what was previously an aspirational model that required unsustainable planning hours became a daily reality with AI-generated individualized plans.

apps.name: Financing Ventures - TerraCycle Case Study
apps.description: Prepares students for an in-class group role-play about business financing using the TerraCycle vermicomposting case.
apps.creator: Gabriela Caso de los Cobos Tapia
apps.usage: Students prepare at home by exploring different investor roles — venture capitalists, angel investors, corporate investors, family members — each with different motivations and risk tolerances. The students who prepared more with the app performed notably better in the live role-play.
apps.impact: Made the gap between preparation and performance visible. Students who engaged deeply with the app showed measurably stronger understanding during the live simulation.

apps.name: DOK Lesson Analyzer
apps.description: Analyzes lessons using Depth of Knowledge frameworks
apps.creator: Al Sylvia
apps.usage: School leaders and teachers analyze lesson plans through the lens of Webb's Depth of Knowledge framework, identifying whether tasks push students toward higher-order thinking or keep them at recall level. The tool provides specific suggestions for increasing cognitive demand.
apps.impact: Made the invisible visible — teachers who thought their lessons were rigorous discovered specific moments where cognitive demand dropped, and leaders could coach toward higher DOK levels with evidence rather than opinion.

apps.name: The Leader You Admire
apps.description: Helps students find and explore a leader who inspires them, then prepare to write about that leader in class.
apps.creator: Gabriela Caso de los Cobos Tapia
apps.usage: Students use the app at home to identify a leader they admire — from public figures to family members. The app helps them develop interview questions, research their leader, and organize their thinking before writing an in-class essay without devices.
apps.impact: Students arrive to class more prepared and less afraid to write. The app does the cognitive heavy lifting outside class so the in-class writing is authentically theirs.

apps.name: Ghana Ministry of Education Lesson Planning Tools
apps.description: AI lesson planning tools for Ghana's national curriculum
apps.creator: Ministry of Education, Ghana
apps.usage: Teachers across Ghana create lesson plans aligned to the national curriculum using AI tools developed in partnership with the Ministry of Education. The platform ensures consistent instructional quality across diverse school contexts.
apps.impact: Brought national curriculum standards to life at the classroom level — teachers in under-resourced schools gained the same quality of lesson planning support as those in well-equipped urban centers.

apps.name: Final Course Reflection
apps.description: Guides students through deep personal reflection on failure, leadership, uncertainty, and growth throughout the course.
apps.creator: Gabriela Caso de los Cobos Tapia
apps.usage: Students engage with probing questions about their experiences, values, and goals. The app pushes them to think critically about what they share — making reflections inherently personal and tied to their own context, which makes generic AI output far less appealing.
apps.impact: Decreased instructor workload while increasing authenticity. Reading genuine app conversations replaced the frustration of evaluating potentially AI-generated essays.

apps.name: Ask Socrates

apps.usage: Students craft open-ended discussion questions with leveled feedback
apps.impact: Transformed Socratic Seminars from conversations dominated by a few voices into discussions where every student arrives prepared

apps.impact: Turned the study of insects from something kids squirm about into something they're fascinated by

apps.impact: Made research methodology engaging by turning it into an adventure where choices have real consequences — students remember the methodology because they lived it rather than memorized it.

apps.name: Interpersonal Speaking Simulation
apps.usage: Students practice real-world conversational scenarios in a safe, low-stakes environment that builds the confidence to speak up in class and beyond. The simulations feel real enough to transfer to actual conversations.
apps.impact: Built the speaking confidence that can only come from practice — students who dreaded being called on in class started volunteering because they'd rehearsed similar conversations with the simulator.

apps.name: TEXAS OIL BOOM CHARACTER CREATION
apps.usage: Students create historical characters
apps.impact: Brought Texas history to life

apps.name: Sudden Illness Role-Playing Scenarios
apps.usage: Students respond to simulated medical emergencies, making split-second decisions that build the instincts needed for real-life situations. Each scenario is different, so students can't just memorize the "right answer."
apps.impact: Prepared students to act rather than freeze in real emergencies — the repeated practice built muscle memory so that when a real situation occurs, the right response is instinctive rather than something they have to think about.

apps.usage: Students practice argumentation
apps.impact: Built structured argumentation skills transferring to writing and civic participation

apps.name: AI Personality Quiz
apps.usage: Students discover insights about their learning preferences and strengths through an engaging personality quiz format. The results spark self-reflection and help students understand how they learn best.
apps.impact: Created self-awareness about learning preferences in a format students actually enjoy — they share results with friends and reference them when choosing study strategies.

apps.usage: Students role-play jurors
apps.impact: Brought legal reasoning and civic engagement to life

apps.usage: Students make narrative choices with consequences
apps.impact: Turned passive reading into active decision-making

apps.usage: Students play and iterate on text-based RPG
apps.impact: Showcases extraordinary student persistence — over 50 iterations of continuous improvement

apps.usage: Students argue cases
apps.impact: Developed legal reasoning and public speaking skills

apps.name: AI Escape Room
apps.usage: Students solve AI-themed challenges in an escape room format, learning about artificial intelligence concepts while exercising problem-solving skills. The puzzles require collaboration and creative thinking.
apps.impact: Taught AI literacy through gameplay — students learned about algorithms, data, and machine learning not from definitions but from solving puzzles that made these concepts tangible and fun.

apps.name: Inventor Bot
apps.usage: Students work through the engineering design process with AI guidance — identifying problems, brainstorming solutions, prototyping ideas, and iterating on designs. The bot coaches young inventors through each stage of turning a creative idea into a workable invention.
apps.impact: Gave students permission to think like engineers and inventors — kids who assumed inventing was for geniuses discovered that the design process is a learnable skill, and their ideas were worth developing.

apps.impact: Built real-world first aid confidence through practice

apps.name: Escape Room: Linear Equations Edition
apps.usage: Students solve equation puzzles in escape room format
apps.impact: Turned equation-solving into a game, motivating disengaged students to persist

apps.name: A Very Angry Quiz Show Host
apps.description: Humorous quiz game with angry host
apps.usage: Students take quizzes hosted by a comedically angry character who adds personality and humor to knowledge assessment. The app was built by a student, showcasing creative game design skills.
apps.impact: Proved that humor and personality in edtech drives engagement — students literally request this app by name, which is the ultimate test of whether a learning tool actually works.

apps.name: Civic Engagement Bot
apps.usage: Students explore civic engagement topics and discover ways they can participate in their communities right now, not just when they turn 18. The bot makes civic action feel accessible and personally relevant.
apps.impact: Encouraged students to see themselves as civic actors today, not just future voters — sparking interest in community involvement that doesn't wait for adulthood.

apps.name: Dungeons and Learnings
apps.usage: Students level up by mastering course content in a D&D-inspired adventure where academic knowledge translates directly into character abilities. The RPG mechanics create intrinsic motivation to learn.
apps.impact: Showed that RPG mechanics can drive genuine academic motivation — students grind for knowledge like gamers grind for XP, voluntarily studying to level up their characters.

apps.usage: NYCPS educators access a dynamic library of student-centered classroom protocols matched to their specific instructional goals. The tool recommends discussion structures, collaborative routines, and engagement strategies based on what the teacher is trying to achieve.
apps.impact: Transformed coaching conversations from theoretical to practical — instead of vague advice to 'be more student-centered,' teachers got specific protocols they could implement in their very next class period.

apps.name: National Math AI Partnership
apps.creator: National Math AI Partnership
apps.usage: Math teachers across the partnership use AI coaching tools to plan and internalize high-quality instructional materials. The suite of apps supports lesson preparation, student thinking anticipation, and instructional decision-making aligned to HQIM standards.
apps.impact: Created a national model for how AI can enhance — not replace — high-quality math curriculum implementation, proving that technology and rigorous pedagogy amplify each other when thoughtfully combined.

apps.name: Personalized Lesson Generator (Kingsman Academy, DC)
apps.description: AI-powered lesson generator aligned to Kingsman Academy's mastery-based learning model, creating individualized learning plans
apps.creator: Kingsman Academy
apps.usage: Teachers create individualized learning plans aligned to Kingsman Academy's mastery-based model, reducing hours of manual planning while maintaining true personalization for every student. The tool generates differentiated content that meets each student exactly where they are.
apps.impact: Made true mastery-based personalization feasible for every teacher at Kingsman — what was previously an aspirational model that required unsustainable planning hours became a daily reality with AI-generated individualized plans.

apps.name: DOK Lesson Analyzer
apps.description: Analyzes lessons using Depth of Knowledge frameworks
apps.creator: Al Sylvia
apps.usage: School leaders and teachers analyze lesson plans through the lens of Webb's Depth of Knowledge framework, identifying whether tasks push students toward higher-order thinking or keep them at recall level. The tool provides specific suggestions for increasing cognitive demand.
apps.impact: Made the invisible visible — teachers who thought their lessons were rigorous discovered specific moments where cognitive demand dropped, and leaders could coach toward higher DOK levels with evidence rather than opinion.

apps.name: Action Step Creator
apps.description: Creates action steps from observations using Get Better Faster, TLAC, T-TESS
apps.creator: Mera Dougherty
apps.usage: Leaders create research-grounded action steps
apps.impact: Transformed post-observation conversations from vague to precise

apps.name: Ghana Ministry of Education Lesson Planning Tools
apps.description: AI lesson planning tools for Ghana's national curriculum
apps.creator: Ministry of Education, Ghana
apps.usage: Teachers across Ghana create lesson plans aligned to the national curriculum using AI tools developed in partnership with the Ministry of Education. The platform ensures consistent instructional quality across diverse school contexts.
apps.impact: Brought national curriculum standards to life at the classroom level — teachers in under-resourced schools gained the same quality of lesson planning support as those in well-equipped urban centers.

apps.name: RACES
apps.description: RACES writing strategy with rubric-aligned feedback
apps.creator: Lars Nelson
apps.usage: Students practice the RACES writing strategy (Restate, Answer, Cite, Explain, Summarize) with AI feedback aligned to their teacher's rubric. The tool coaches students through each step of constructing evidence-based written responses.
apps.impact: Gave students a structured framework that made evidence-based writing feel achievable rather than overwhelming — students who froze at open-ended prompts started producing organized, well-supported responses with confidence.

apps.impact: Broke down the communication barriers that kept many Wayside families from engaging with their child's school — parents who felt intimidated by the system now have a welcoming, always-available guide.

apps.impact: Enabled teachers to create unlimited practice questions rather than reusing the same released tests — students got better preparation because they practiced with novel questions rather than memorizing old ones.

apps.impact: Made professional development something teachers engage with rather than endure — the interactive format sparked genuine reflection instead of the glazed-over compliance typical of traditional PD days.

apps.name: [REMIX] STAAR Math Reflection App
apps.usage: Texas students reflect on their STAAR math performance through guided analysis of their reasoning and mistakes. The tool turns test results into learning opportunities rather than just numbers on a report.
apps.impact: Transformed test results from dead-end disappointments into launching pads for growth — students who used to crumple up their score reports now use them as study guides.

apps.name: Elevating your Speaking - TELPAS
apps.usage: Students develop the oral language skills that the TELPAS speaking assessment demands, practicing with a patient conversation partner that gives them unlimited tries. The tool focuses on the specific speaking competencies that are hardest to practice without a dedicated partner.
apps.impact: Built the oral language skills that are nearly impossible to develop without a patient conversation partner — students who never spoke in class started finding their voice because they could practice without an audience.

apps.impact: Showed that national curriculum materials work best when they're locally adapted — Compass Rose students got Amplify's rigor with their school's personality baked in.

apps.name: Gary: Mr. Hank's Spanish 1 Tutor
apps.usage: Students practice Spanish conversation and grammar with Gary, getting the kind of immersive speaking practice that classroom time alone can never provide enough of. The bot adapts to each student's level, making practice feel like conversation rather than drill.
apps.impact: Gave Spanish 1 students unlimited conversation practice — the one thing every language teacher wishes they could provide more of but can't in a class of 30 students with 50-minute periods.

apps.name: Spanish Verb Generator
apps.usage: Students practice conjugation across tenses and skill levels, with exercises that adapt to where they are in their learning journey. The tool generates fresh practice sets each time, so students never just memorize answers.
apps.impact: Made verb conjugation practice adaptive rather than one-size-fits-all — students who were drowning in irregular verbs got focused practice on exactly what they needed, while advanced students were pushed further.

apps.name: ESL Sentence Master

apps.impact: Built the sentence-level confidence that opens the door to everything else in English writing — students who couldn't construct a complete sentence started writing paragraphs because they finally had the structural foundation.

apps.impact: Provided the precisely leveled instruction that intermediate ELLs need most — they've outgrown beginner supports but aren't ready for mainstream content, and this tool met them exactly where they are.

apps.impact: Demonstrated that AI can support indigenous language preservation and instruction — students learning Asante Twi gained a digital companion that honored their cultural heritage while building literacy in their mother tongue.
